Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Exclude Plaintiffs Expert Thomas Gourley, M.D. And Report Of Daniel Powers, M.D.
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Ronald J. Wilson

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO EXCLUDE PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT THOMAS GOURLEY, M.D. AND REPORT OF DANIEL POWERS, M.D.

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling on defendant Golden Gate Drywall's motion for protective order to exclude plaintiff's expert Thomas Gourley, M.D. and report of Daniel Powers, M.D.: The report of Dr. Powers is ordered excluded. Plaintiff's request for a continuance and to reopen discovery is denied, no good cause shown. Dr. Gourley is allowed to testify but he cannot rely on Dr. Powers' report. Plaintiff's opposition is procedurally improper because it violates C.R.C. Rule 3.1113(d)(f) and seeks relief that cannot be granted without a separate motion. Plaintiff's opposition is nineteen pages long and does not contain a table of contents or authorities. Additionally, plaintiff's opposition seeks a continuance of the trial date and to reopen experts discovery. However, the rules of Court explicitly require that a party seeking a continuance of a trial date must make the request by "a noticed motion or an ex parte application." Furthermore, the Court's April 22 Order re-opening expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing Dr. Bruch and allowing any party to obtain plaintiff's updated medical records, all other discovery remains closed. The Court finds that these are violations of defendant's due process and the discovery rules. The Court may impose an evidence sanction by an order prohibiting any party engaging in the misuse of the discovery process from introducing designated matters in evidence. (C.C.P. 2023.030(c)). Misuses of the discovery process include disobeying a court order. (C.C.P. 2023.010). Counsel for defendant is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
